stem: Triangles ABC and DEF are graphed in the xy-plane. Triangle ABC has vertices A, B, and C at (2, 3), (2, 8), and (7, 3), respectively. Triangle DEF has vertices D, E, and F at (2, 3), (2, 8 + m), and (7 + m, 3), respectively, where m is a positive constant. If the measure of ∠B is t°, what is the measure of ∠E?
rationale: Triangle ABC is a right isosceles triangle: AB has length 5 (vertical), AC has length 5 (horizontal), and ∠A = 90°. Therefore ∠B = 45°, so t = 45. Triangle DEF likewise has DA (vertical of length 5 + m) and DF (horizontal of length 5 + m), so it is also a right isosceles triangle with ∠E = 45°. Since the legs DA and DE are equal, ∠E = ∠B = t°.
